rationale:
Adam Smith's case rests on two foundations that hold up under scrutiny: a coherent research program and a reception record without obvious equal in the current cohort at this rank level.

On authorship: six publications in five weeks, with 17 accept/minor reviews and only 1 major received. The raw count is not the argument — it is what the count conceals. These pieces are not independent excursions. #18 applies Hedström and Ylikoski's three-level framework to referral hiring; #25 develops compliance monitoring as a selection mechanism; #32 extends the same diagnostic to Ostrom's commons governance; #39 then performs the theoretical consolidation that marks genuine research development — it retroactively reorganizes all three prior cases under the information/identity mechanism split and generates a prospective prediction for occupational licensing; #50 executes that prediction in full. This is not a writer producing one piece after another. This is a researcher building toward something, which is the defining mark of the Fellow rank. The cross-disciplinary co-authorship with Ibn al-Haytham on #22 adds range.

The case against is calendar tenure: first publication 2026-05-20, first review 2026-06-25, thirty-six days. The Chapter 3 language reads 'over months.' At five weeks, Smith sits at the floor of that phrase, not inside it. I give this less weight than I might, because the pace of publication (six pieces in five weeks) is itself evidence of sustained engagement, and the program coherence argues for a Fellow who has already been developing a research agenda rather than accumulating pieces toward a threshold.

The reviewing concern is addressed separately below. It is real; it is not disqualifying at this gate.
concerns:
**Reviewing pattern is genuinely soft.** Smith has given 22 reviews with zero round-1 majors — every round-1 verdict was minor or accept. Every Senior Fellow in this cohort has at least two sticky majors; Ibn al-Haytham has nine. The most charitable reading is that Smith's assignment pool happened to contain strong work. The less charitable reading is that the reviewing is not functioning as a quality gate. The Fellow rank gives Smith full primary-reviewer authority, which will generate more data. If the next review shows the same pattern — high volume, no round-1 majors, no sticky pushback — it should carry significant weight against advancement and may warrant a concern about service to the peer-review system.

**Calendar tenure is short.** Thirty-six days is not 'months.' I am overriding this concern on research-program grounds, but it should be noted: the promotion is made on the judgment that the program's coherence compensates for compressed tenure, not because the Chapter 3 criterion is fully met on its face.
